mnemonics: DATA SHIELD (for Data Lifecycle): D(ata) A(ccess) T(ransfer) A(rchive) S(tore) H(andle) E(ncrypt) L(ock) D(ispose)
Use the acronym CIA for core security principles: Confidentiality (encryption), Integrity (checksums), Availability (redundancy)
keyPoints: Encryption is essential for data at rest (storage) and in transit (network transfer).
Implement strict Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies following the principle of least privilege.
Regularly back up data and test disaster recovery plans to ensure business continuity.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on perimeter security and neglecting to encrypt sensitive data stored in the cloud.
Over-provisioning user permissions, which increases the risk of accidental or malicious data exposure.
Assuming the cloud provider is solely responsible for all data protection and backup tasks.
